Failure Scenario: Train sleep at mainline more than 2 times and able to wake up
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: N/A Train already in service: Wake remotely or manual intervention Exit during Fleet Reduction
Local Response: Maintenance staff to check and verify the situation.

Failure Scenario: Foreign object on track (depending on the type/size of foreign object)
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: N/A Train already in service:TO verify the obstruction on the track.
Local Response: TO verify the obstruction on the track. If major failure occurred, required maintenance team to attend. De-energized certain area if required

Failure Scenario: Failure of 2 TMS Central Control Unit (CCU)
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: Not allowed to enter service Train already in service: Immediate Train Withdrawal from service Note: Rescue Train may be required
Local Response: Reset CCU. If not success, train need to be couple

Failure Scenario: Failure of Double Multifunction Vehicle Bus (MVB) Network
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: Not allowed to enter service Train already in service: Immediate Train Withdrawal from service Note: Rescue Train may be required
Local Response: Check on HMI and identify which equipment causing alarm appear and reset if possible

Failure Scenario: Failure of 2 Current Collector Shoes on 1 car on 1 side
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: Not allowed to enter service Train already in service: Immediate Train Withdrawal from service Note: Deploy qualified Train driver to verify the fault at the next station upon receiving the alarm
Train Entering service: Not allowed to enter service Train already in service: Immediate Train Withdrawal from service.
Local Response: i. Single red APS alarm "“Reset APS. If unsuccessful, immediate train removal . ii. Both APS Failure- Reset APS.If unsuccesful, immediate train removal.

Guideline for Chief Controller: i. Bypass dedicated feeder breaker and feeder isolator. ii. Closed on-load isolator 89L21 / 89L31.
Local Response: Maintenance technician to verify condition breaker at site: i. Reset breaker and inform OCC to normalize. ii. Unable to reset, remain bypass and to be attend during EP hour.

Failure Scenario: Failure of 2 TMS Central Control Unit (CCU)
Guideline for Chief Controller: Train Entering service: Not allowed to enter service Train already in service: Immediate Train Withdrawal from service Note: Rescue Train may be required
Local Response: Reset CCU. If not success, train need to be couple
